_warning: HAND-AUTHORED. A sync script that writes this file is a bug. No upstream source has EVENTS as entities — the duels, wars, deaths and declarations exist only inside chapter prose — so every row here is hand-typed. verified:false means Shawn has not confirmed the chapters against the manga yet.
_why_this_file_exists: The atlas charts WHERE things are as of a chapter; this file charts WHAT HAPPENED there. api-onepiece.com and the wiki have arcs and debuts, but no on-page event with a chapter stamp and a place. The Zoro–Mihawk duel, the fall of Arlong Park, the war at Marineford — these are the beats the whole map exists to locate, and nobody ships them as data.
_semantics: occurred_chapter is when the READER sees the event on the page — the fruit_reveals rule, not in-world truth. Roger's execution happened decades before chapter 1 in-world; the reader watches it in the chapter 1 prologue, so occurred_chapter is 1. through_chapter (nullable) closes a multi-chapter beat; the event is chapter-gated on occurred_chapter alone. Position: island_slug resolves via canon/islands.coords.json, OR an explicit lng/lat for open-sea events (the Baratie is a floating restaurant with no island row — same convention as voyage_legs and presence windows). An event must never occur on an island the reader has not charted: normalize.py refuses occurred_chapter < the island's debut_chapter.
_kinds: duel = a formal one-on-one. battle = a fight that decides an arc. war = a multi-front conflict. declaration = words that change the world on the page. death = an on-page death. execution = a public execution (attempted or carried out). oath = a promise the story keeps returning to. escape = a getaway that closes a beat. departure = a crossing or setting-out that moves the voyage itself.
_significance: 1 = arc beat. 2 = saga-defining. 3 = era-defining (the map should whisper about it).
_participants: Character slugs from data/canon.json characters[], validated by normalize.py. role is a short free label ('victor', 'defeated', 'duelist', 'executed'). Crews and armies are named in prose via outcome, not as rows — a participant here is a person the reader watched.
_before_any_content_ships: Every row is verified:false. Shawn must confirm every occurred_chapter against the manga and flip verified to true. Until then the UI renders these as unverified.

events.name: The Straw Hat Promise
events.outcome: Shanks leaves his straw hat with Luffy — to be returned when Luffy becomes a great pirate. The whole voyage is this promise being kept.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 1 (Romance Dawn): Shanks gives Luffy the straw hat as he departs Foosha Village. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Execution of Gol D. Roger
events.outcome: The Pirate King dies at Loguetown — and his last words about the One Piece ignite the Great Pirate Era. In-world this is decades before the story; the reader watches it in the chapter 1 prologue, so it is charted from chapter 1.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 1 prologue: Roger's execution at Loguetown and the words that launch the era. occurred_chapter follows the reader-time rule. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Fall of Axe-Hand Morgan
events.outcome: Luffy and Zoro topple the tyrant of the 153rd Marine Branch — the crew's first fight as a crew.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Shells Town: Morgan is beaten and Zoro sails with Luffy (~ch. 6). The exact defeat chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Buggy Blasted Away at Orange Town
events.outcome: Nami ties Buggy's scattered parts together and Luffy sends the rest flying — Orange Town is free, and the map's longest-running grudge begins.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Orange Town: Luffy defeats Buggy with Nami's help (~ch. 20). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Kuro of a Hundred Plans Falls
events.outcome: The butler's three-year plan dies on the beach; Syrup Village never learns it was ever in danger, because Usopp the liar tells them nothing happened.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Syrup Village: Luffy defeats Captain Kuro (~ch. 39). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Zoro vs. Mihawk — the Duel at the Baratie
events.outcome: The world's greatest swordsman opens Zoro's chest and spares his life for his ambition. Zoro's vow to Luffy — never to lose again until he takes the title — is the oath the whole swordsman's arc hangs on.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. The Baratie, open sea (~ch. 50–51): Mihawk defeats Zoro; the never-again vow follows. Explicit lng/lat: the Baratie is a floating restaurant with no island row; position matches the voyage's Baratie anchor. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Don Krieg Defeated at the Baratie
events.outcome: The self-styled strongest armada of East Blue breaks against one rubber pirate defending a floating restaurant; Sanji sails soon after.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. The Baratie: Luffy defeats Don Krieg (~ch. 66). Explicit lng/lat: the Baratie has no island row; position matches the voyage's Baratie anchor. The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Fall of Arlong Park
events.outcome: Luffy brings Arlong Park down on its owner's head. Eight years of Cocoyasi's tribute end, and Nami joins the crew for real — the hat on her head while she cried is the hinge of East Blue.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Conomi Islands: Luffy defeats Arlong and the Park collapses (~ch. 93). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Escape from the Execution Platform
events.outcome: On the platform where Roger died, Buggy's blade falls — and lightning falls first. Luffy grins where the Pirate King grinned, and Smoker decides this one is his to chase.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Loguetown: the execution attempt and the lightning strike (~ch. 99). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Into the Grand Line
events.outcome: The barrel oath in the storm, and the Going Merry rides Reverse Mountain up and over — East Blue ends, the Grand Line begins.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Reverse Mountain (~ch. 100–101): the crew's declarations over the barrel and the ascent into the Grand Line. Charted at ch. 101 with the island's debut. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Luffy Punches Alvida
events.outcome: One rubber punch ends Alvida's rule of her own deck, and Coby is free to chase the Marines. The story's first fight: a nobody with a straw hat knocks a pirate captain off her ship with one blow.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 2: Luffy defeats Alvida with a single Gomu Gomu no Pistol aboard her ship; the deck fight has no canon coordinate, so the position is the hand-authored ship-at-sea anchor shared with the scene pack. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Nami Asks for Help
events.outcome: Nami stabs the tattoo that owns her and finally says the words: help me. Luffy puts his hat on her head and walks toward Arlong Park. The sentence that turns a navigator into a crewmate.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 81 'Tears': Nami's plea on the Cocoyasi village road and Luffy's answer. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Zoro vs. a Hundred Bounty Hunters
events.outcome: The welcoming town drops its mask at midnight and a hundred Baroque Works bounty hunters draw on one swordsman. Zoro cuts through the whole roster across the rooftops of Whisky Peak — the Grand Line's first lesson that the crew's monsters are real.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapters 107-108: Zoro against the Baroque Works agents of Whisky Peak at night. Charted at ch. 107 where the fight begins. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Miss All Sunday Appears
events.outcome: The partner of Baroque Works' boss materializes on the Going Merry's deck, names the crew's danger, and offers an easy way out. Luffy throws her Eternal Pose overboard. The future is standing on their ship and nobody knows it yet.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 114: Miss All Sunday's appearance aboard the Going Merry off Whisky Peak. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Ace Blocks Smoker at Nanohana
events.outcome: Smoke meets fire in a Nanohana street: Ace stands between Smoker and his little brother, two Logias cancelling each other while the Straw Hats run for the harbor. The getaway that introduces the brother Luffy sails the Grand Line beside.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 158: Ace intercepts Smoker in Nanohana so the crew can escape to the Going Merry. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Fire Fist Burns the Billions Fleet
events.outcome: Five Baroque Works ships move on the Going Merry off Nanohana, and Ace answers with one attack. The Hiken crosses the water, the fleet becomes burning timber, and the name Fire Fist explains itself.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 159: Ace destroys the Billions convoy with Hiken during the departure from Nanohana. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Luffy vs. Crocodile — Round One
events.outcome: In the desert outside Rainbase, rubber meets sand and loses. Crocodile takes Luffy apart without effort, hooks him, and leaves him to the quicksand — the first opponent the Grand Line sends that Luffy simply cannot hit.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapters 176-179: the first Luffy-Crocodile fight in the desert near Rainbase, ending in Luffy's defeat. Charted at ch. 176 where the fight begins. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Sanji vs. Bon Clay
events.outcome: Ballet kenpo against the Black Leg on the Alubarna plateau: Bon Clay's copied faces meet a fighter who never needed to see the face at all. Sanji wins the duel and the crew's path into the capital stays open.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapters 187-189: Sanji defeats Mr. 2 Bon Clay outside Alubarna. Charted at ch. 187 where the duel begins. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Zoro vs. Mr. 1
events.outcome: A swordsman who cannot be cut against blades that cut everything. In the streets of Alubarna, Zoro learns to hear the breath of steel and cuts the steel man — the duel that turns a promise to Kuina into a technique.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapters 194-195: Zoro defeats Mr. 1 (Das Bones) in Alubarna, learning to cut steel. Charted at ch. 194 where the focused duel opens. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Nami vs. Miss Doublefinger
events.outcome: The navigator who always ran finally stands her ground. With a weapon she barely understands and weather she understands completely, Nami builds heat and cold into a tornado over Alubarna and wins her first real fight alone.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapters 190-193: Nami defeats Miss Doublefinger with the Clima-Tact in Alubarna. Charted at ch. 190 where the fight begins. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Luffy vs. Crocodile — Round Two
events.outcome: Luffy comes back carrying water — the one answer sand cannot ignore. Wet fists finally land, and for the first time Crocodile bleeds; then the poison hook ends the round. Losing twice is the lesson; the third fight is the answer.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapters 198-201: the second Luffy-Crocodile fight; water-armed Luffy wounds Crocodile but falls to the poison hook. Charted at ch. 198 where the rematch begins. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Luffy vs. Crocodile — the Final Round
events.outcome: Poisoned, bleeding, and past every limit, Luffy beats the sand with his own blood on his fists. The Gum-Gum Storm drives Crocodile up through the bedrock into the sky over Alubarna — and the rain returns to Arabasta.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapters 203-210: the final Luffy-Crocodile battle beneath and above Alubarna, ending the war and the drought. Charted at ch. 203 where the last round begins. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Luffy vs. Enel — the Golden Bell
events.outcome: Lightning meets the one body it cannot rule. Luffy carries the golden weight back up Giant Jack, defeats Enel with the final golden strike, and rings Shandora's bell across the sky.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored from official VIZ chapter 279 and volume 30 receipts; the fight begins at chapter 279 and reaches its bell resolution by chapter 302. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Luffy vs. Blueno — Gear Second
events.outcome: On the courthouse roof, Luffy unveils Gear Second. Blueno's Door-Door evasions and Iron Body cannot contain the new speed, and the first Jet-series barrage opens the road deeper into Enies Lobby.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored from official VIZ chapter 387 and the official ONE PIECE.com Blueno profile; the fight resolves through chapter 388. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Sanji vs. Jabra — First Diable Jambe
events.outcome: Jabra's wolf-form Iron Body meets a kick hot enough to break through it. Sanji ignites Diable Jambe for the first time and finishes the CP9 fighter with the flaming combination.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored from official VIZ chapter 415 and the official ONE PIECE.com Jabra profile. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Zoro vs. Kaku — Asura
events.outcome: Kaku's giraffe-hybrid Four-Sword Style fills the tower with Tempest Kicks. Zoro answers by manifesting Asura and ending the duel with the nine-sword strike.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored from official VIZ chapters 416-417 and the official ONE PIECE.com Kaku profile. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Luffy vs. Rob Lucci — Jet Gatling
events.outcome: Luffy burns through Gear Second and Gear Third to keep Lucci away from Robin and the crew. The last Jet Gatling breaks CP9's strongest fighter and leaves Luffy standing only because his crew is waiting.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored from official VIZ chapter 418 and volume 43 receipts; the duel begins at chapter 418 and ends with the chapter-427 escape beat. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
